headline: One-command bash audit pipeline that runs 6 drift checks against Atlas-the-system, emits JSONL findings + markdown report + diffable snapshot, and exits with the count of NEW findings for scheduler/directive consumption.

capabilities.invoke: bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # check 1: reserved device-name files (NUL/CON/...)
bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # check 2: imported-but-untracked atlas/*.ts (git ls-files)
bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # check 3: Record<Mode,...> partial 6-mode coverage
bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # check 4: atlas/ LOC>100 with 0 test files
bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # checks 5+6: missing mode_since + untracked long doctrine .md
bash tools/atlas-audit/audit.sh  # writes findings-/report-/snapshot-<date> to runs/
bash audit.sh && jq ... findings-$(date -u +%F).json | curl -X POST localhost:3001/api/atlas/directives
